title: Experiment Metrics
description: Schema for per-experiment metrics.json output. Each experiment run produces one metrics.json file in its result directory, capturing the experiment identity, seed, timing, hardware, and measured metrics.

experiment_name.description: Unique identifier for this experiment run (e.g., 'ablation_no_memory_seed42')

experiment_type.description: Category of experiment: benchmark (baseline measurement), ablation (component removal), comparison (method vs method), validation (correctness check)

seed.description: Random seed used for this run. All RNGs (Python, NumPy, PyTorch) are pinned to this value.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the experiment execution began

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the experiment execution finished

hardware.description: Hardware and environment snapshot captured at experiment start

cpu.description: CPU model or architecture identifier (e.g., 'arm', 'x86_64', 'Apple M2')

cpu_count.description: Number of logical CPU cores available

ram_gb.description: Total system RAM in gigabytes. Null if detection failed.

gpu.description: GPU model and memory, or 'none' if no GPU detected

cuda_version.description: CUDA toolkit version string, or null if no CUDA available

metrics.description: Measured metrics from this experiment run. Keys are metric names, values are numeric results. Structure varies per experiment type.

primary_metric.description: The single most important metric for this experiment, used for keep/discard decisions in overnight mode

name.description: Metric name matching a key in the metrics object

value.description: Numeric value of the primary metric

direction.description: Whether higher or lower values indicate better performance
